title: rubus adenocomus
side_effects: Rubus adenocomus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. In severe cases, consuming large amounts of the plant can lead to kidney damage, nausea, and vomiting. Additionally, the plant's high levels of anthraquinones may cause stomach cramps, diarrhea, and electrolyte imbalance if used as a laxative. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Rubus adenocomus for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
